pages.raw_text:
Are western railroads, particular
ly the Northern Pacific and Union
Pacific deliberately limiting the
fuel supply of this and other cities
of their territory until cold wealth
er creates an enormous demand
and consequent jump in prices
This Is the question looming up
over the Spokane fuel situation.


OF the mines that supply Spo-
kane with coal, the Roslyn is own
ed by the Northern Pacific and the
Rock Springs is the property Of the
Union Pacific. Other mines that
send coal here are the Monarch,
Wyoming, owned by the Wyoming
Coal Mining company; Carney, also
in Wyoming, owned by the Carney
Coal Mining company. and Crows
Nest, owned by the Crows Nest
Coal Mining company. Whether
these are Independent concerns or
subsidiary companies Of the rail.
roads interested is of course mat
ter unknown except to the princi-
pals.


But the fact remains that the
whole wide western territory,
served by both the Northern Pa
cific and the Hartman lines. have
been absolutely drained Of fuel CO.
incident with the approach of cold
weather. There has been no Rock
Springs coal on the local market
for long time. One car came in
yesterday from Roslyn.


The wood supply that might
have relieved the situation-and
likewise counteracted in a great
measure the effects of coal
famine-has beeN withheld by lack
of cars. II the prevailing scarcity
of coal is the result of deliberate
arrangement, the cutting off OF
the wood supply naturally remain
ed merely a matter Of distributing
rolling stock so that in such man
ner that it would not be available.
-~Iu Omaha yesterday grand
Jury returned 81 indictments
against a coal firm for violation of
the antitrust law. Ogden has
been suffering for fuel for some
time. The coal famine is as acute,
apparently. in one place as in an
other, and nicely apportioned
everywhere. There Is just enough
available always to keep every
body nervous and reconciled to the
prospect Of famine prices. The
railroads blame the mines-and
